content.text:
<user_query>
@/Users/HanHu/Downloads/[XA 103905] CU-5 M3_ motion_rl 上手指南 之 humanoid-gym 篇.pdf 

In an evaluation script, according to the PDF document as a guide, the environment to run is the Isaac Chen container that you need to remote access into.
</user_query>

I’ll first read the PDF you linked to extract the exact evaluation workflow, then I can map that to what needs to run inside the Isaac Chen remote container and give you a concrete command sequence/script.

**Preparing to extract PDF instructions**

I’ve extracted the key steps from the PDF. Next I’ll quickly check whether your local workspace already has a `motion_rl` checkout and the expected scripts, so I can give you an exact evaluation command/script for the Isaac Chen container flow.

**Planning container evaluation setup**

Understood. Per that PDF, **evaluation must run inside the remote Isaac container (`isaacgym`)**, not directly on your local Mac.

Use this flow:

```bash
# 1) SSH to Isaac Chen machine
ssh <your_user>@<isaac_chen_host>

# 2) Enter motion_rl repo on remote host
cd /path/to/motion_rl

# 3) Start/enter Isaac container (choose isaacgym when prompted)
bash scripts/docker_run.sh

# 4) Inside container, switch to humanoid-gym
cd /home/gymuser/software/motion_rl/humanoid-gym

# 5) Run evaluation (play)
python humanoid/scripts/play.py --task r01_plus_amp_with_4dof_arms --load_run=example
```

If you need CPU-only eval (no GPU), use:

```bash
python humanoid/scripts/play.py --task r01_plus_amp_with_4dof_arms --load_run=example --rl_device cpu --sim_device cpu
```

If you want, I can write a ready-to-use `eval_remote.sh` wrapper for you (SSH + docker/container entry + eval command in one shot) once you share:
- remote host/IP
- SSH user
- repo path on remote machine
- task name and checkpoint/load_run.

**Providing remote evaluation guidance**

<user_query>
Write me all the sequences of commands I can paste into a terminal and run this on my own.Assume you have access to the host machine that the container is on.
</user_query>

Perfect—here are **paste-ready command sequences** for the host machine.

> Assumptions:
> - You can run commands on the Linux host where Docker/GPU is available.
> - `motion_rl` repo already exists on that host.
> - You want to evaluate in the `isaacgym` container per the PDF.

## 1) One-time host precheck (optional but recommended)

```bash
nvidia-smi
docker --version
nvidia-ctk --version
docker run --rm --gpus all nvidia/cuda:11.0.3-base-ubuntu20.04 nvidia-smi
```

---

## 2) Start container and run evaluation (pretrained example checkpoint)

Set your repo path first, then run:

```bash
export MOTION_RL_DIR="/absolute/path/to/motion_rl"
cd "$MOTION_RL_DIR"

# if needed for image pull
docker login --username=xrobot-infra-acr@xmotors-ai xrobot-infra-registry.cn-wulanchabu.cr.aliyuncs.com
# (enter password when prompted)

# start/enter container (select isaacgym if prompted)
bash scripts/docker_run.sh
```

Now you are inside the container. Run:

```bash
cd /home/gymuser/software/motion_rl/humanoid-gym
mkdir -p logs/r01_plus_amp_with_4dof_arms/example

# optional: download provided checkpoint from doc
curl -L "https://xrobot.xiaopeng.link/resource/xrobot-log/user-upload/fuyao/guomh/bifrost-2025051107240410-guomh/model_15000.pt" \
  -o logs/r01_plus_amp_with_4dof_arms/example/model_15000.pt

# evaluate
python humanoid/scripts/play.py --task r01_plus_amp_with_4dof_arms --load_run=example
```

---

## 3) Evaluate your own trained run

Inside container:

```bash
cd /home/gymuser/software/motion_rl/humanoid-gym
python humanoid/scripts/play.py --task r01_plus_amp_with_4dof_arms --load_run=<your_run_dir_name>
```

Example `load_run` is a folder under:
`logs/r01_plus_amp_with_4dof_arms/`

---

## 4) CPU-only fallback evaluation (if GPU unavailable)

Inside container:

```bash
cd /home/gymuser/software/motion_rl/humanoid-gym
python humanoid/scripts/play.py --task r01_plus_amp_with_4dof_arms --load_run=example --rl_device cpu --sim_device cpu
```

---

## 5) Fully non-interactive single paste (host → exec running container)

Use this if container `isaacgym` is already up (or started by `docker_run.sh` before this):

```bash
export MOTION_RL_DIR="/absolute/path/to/motion_rl"
cd "$MOTION_RL_DIR"
bash scripts/docker_run.sh
docker exec -it isaacgym bash -lc '
cd /home/gymuser/software/motion_rl/humanoid-gym &&
python humanoid/scripts/play.py --task r01_plus_amp_with_4dof_arms --load_run=example
'
```

---

If you want, I can also give you a **single `run_eval.sh` file** that does: precheck + container startup + checkpoint fetch + play in one command.

**Preparing copy-paste container commands**
